Subject: Re: Urgent Issue: API Authentication Failing After IAM Role Update
Body:
Dear Isabella,

Thank you for contacting Aetheros Support. We understand the urgency of this issue and apologize for the inconvenience it's causing.

To help us diagnose the problem, could you please provide the following information:

* The specific IAM role that was updated.
* The timestamp of when the issue first started occurring.
* A sample API request and response (please redact any sensitive information).

Once we have this information, we'll be able to investigate further and assist you in resolving this issue.

Best regards,
The Aetheros Support Team

Subject: Identity Update [Account - Information]: Receipt # 3054812024.!!
Body: PP by Poky Reference #PP-005-459-967-002 Account Status Update Response required We may ask you to confirm information Upon receipt Log in to your PayPal account to resolve a limitation Dear customer, Recently, there's been activity in your PayPal account that seems unusual compared to your normal account activities. Please log in to PayPal to confirm your identity. To help protect your account, no one can send money or withdraw money. In addition, no one can add money to your account, add a card, add a bank account, remove any bank accounts, remove credit cards, send refunds, or close your account. What to do Log in to your PayPal account as soon as possible. We may ask you to confirm information you provided when you created your account to make sure you're the account holder.Â You should also do the following for your own protection: Check your account detailsÂ (address, email, phone, etc.) to make sure they're accurate. What's next? After you complete all of the tasks, we should respond within 72 hours. If you need help or have any questions, call us at 0800 942 890, From 8:00 to 20:30, Monday to Friday, From 10:00 to 18:30, Saturday and Sunday. Sincerely, PayPal

Subject: Re: Inquiry about API development and IAM integration
Body:
Dear John,

Thank you for your interest in Aetheros! We're happy to provide more information about our API development and IAM services.

You can find detailed documentation on our website about how to integrate our IAM service with custom APIs. We also have several pre-built connectors and libraries that can simplify the integration process.

Let me know if you have other questions.

Best regards,
The Aetheros Support Team
text:
Re: Inquiry about API development and IAM integration

Dear John,

Thank you for your interest in Aetheros! We're happy to provide more information about our API development and IAM services.

You can find detailed documentation on our website about how to integrate our IAM service with custom APIs. We also have several pre-built connectors and libraries that can simplify the integration process.

Let me know if you have other questions.

Best regards,
The Aetheros Support Team

Subject: Adobe Illustrator Crashed All of a Sudden
Body: I've been encountering a persistent problem with Adobe Illustrator CC 2021. It crashes unexpectedly, causing a significant disruption in my work process. I believe my old graphics driver is the root of this issue. After attempting to restart my computer, updating the software, and verifying my system resources, the issue remains unresolved. I haven't updated my graphics driver because I'm not sure about the correct procedure. I would be grateful for your guidance on how to fix this, including step-by-step instructions or suggestions for compatible driver updates for my system.
text:
Adobe Illustrator Crashed All of a Sudden

I've been encountering a persistent problem with Adobe Illustrator CC 2021. It crashes unexpectedly, causing a significant disruption in my work process. I believe my old graphics driver is the root of this issue. After attempting to restart my computer, updating the software, and verifying my system resources, the issue remains unresolved. I haven't updated my graphics driver because I'm not sure about the correct procedure. I would be grateful for your guidance on how to fix this, including step-by-step instructions or suggestions for compatible driver updates for my system.

Subject: HR News — Save on your taxes with the home office tax deduction
Body: News Tax Deduction Home office tax deduction With tax season upon us, we want to share tax-related information with our remote and hybrid employees. As an employee who worked from home at least a day per week last year, you are eligible to claim a tax deduction for some home office expenses. This includes office supplies, phone and internet expenses. The home office tax deduction can be claimed on your personal income tax return. If you would like to benefit from this tax advantage, use our tax deduction calculator to estimate the amount you can claim. All the best, Your HR Team

Subject: [UAI] 2004 IEEE International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Systems
Body: **** Please apologize for multiple copies **** Second Call for Papers AISTA 2004 --> 2004 IEEE International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Systems - Theory and Applications November 15-18, 2004 --> Centre de Recherche Public Henri Tudor, Luxembourg-Kirchberg, Luxembourg http://aista.tudor.lu/aista2004 IMPORTANT DATES: Submission of extended summaries: September 5, 2004 Notification of acceptance: September 26, 2004 Camera-ready paper due: October 24, 2004 AISTA Conference: November 15-18, 2004 AIMS and SCOPE: The International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Systems - Theory and Applications - AISTA'2004 aims at bringing together theoreticians and practitioners of Intelligent Systems from Luxembourg and around the world to exchange ideas, present their recent results and discuss possible collaborations. The focus of the conference includes, but is not limited=20 to the areas listed below. * Control Systems: Neural Networks Control, Fuzzy Logic Control, Genetic Algorithms & Evolutionary Control, Model-Predictive Control, Adaptive and Optimal Control, Intelligent Control Systems, Robotics and Automation, Fault Diagnosis, Intelligent agents & Multiagent Systems, Industrial Automations Hybrid Systems; Fuzzy Evolutionary Systems, Fuzzy Expert Systems, Fuzzy Neural Systems, Neural Genetic Systems, Neural-Fuzzy-Genetic Systems, Hybrid Systems for Optimization. * Optimization and Prediction: Multi-criteria Optimization, Signal Processing, Time Series Analysis & Prediction, System Identification, Data Mining & Knowledge Discovery, Intelligent Information Systems, Image Processing, Parallel Computing applications in Identification & Control, Pattern Recognition, Clustering, Classification. * Decision Making: Intelligent Databases & Information Retrieval, Case-Based Reasoning, Decision Analysis, Dynamic Systems Modeling, Decision Support Systems, Qualitative and Approximate-Reasoning Modeling. * Security: Intrusion Detection, Privilege Determining, Denial-of-Service Resistance, Attack Resistance, Cryptographic Mechanisms and Protocols, Mobile Environment Security, Wireless Security, Access control, Database=20 Security, Identification and Authentication. * Workshop: A workshop dedicated to Satellite Control and Communications will take place in parallel to the conference sessions. Topics might come for=20 example from the field of Intelligent Ground Control Systems, Autonomous Satellite Cluster Control, Intelligent Telemetry Processing, and Intelligent Antenna Systems. SUBMISSION OF PAPERS Papers reporting original and unpublished research or application results pertaining to the above and related topics are solicited. Papers will be reviewed on the basis of two pages extended abstracts of sufficient detail to permit reasonable evaluation. Accepted papers will appear in the conference proceedings with an ISBN. The final version of accepted papers should be of six pages long. Submissions should include the title, author(s), affiliation(s), e-mail address(es), tel/fax numbers, postal address(es) and keywords on the first page. Papers should be submitted on the conference web site: http://aista.tudor.lu/aista2004. If web=20 submission is not possible, manuscripts should be sent as an attachment via email to one of the Program Chairs listed below on before the deadline date of September 5, 2004. A special issue of the international of Integrated=20 Computer-Aided Engineering (ICAE) will be devoted to publish extended versions of the=20 best papers=20 presented at AISTA'04. Subject: FIDO's, WebAuthn's Post-Quantum Future
Body:
[1]
[2]
FIDO's, WebAuthn's Post-Quantum Future
**
Fortunately, in the last 12 months we have seen a massive increase in the popularity and adoption of FIDO and WebAuthn. Now most major websites support this new, convenient and secure authentication method that aims to replace ordinary passwords.
But what happens if one day in the future a quantum computer breaks the underlying cryptography, potentially rendering this authentication method insecure? To mitigate this threat, it is important to have post-quantum (PQ) cryptography in place in time. As one of the first steps, SandboxAQ introduces the first end-to-end post-quantum secure None [3] implementation of the FIDO2 protocol.
The aim of this open-source project is to provide a complete implementation that allows post-quantum secure end-to-end registration and authentication in all steps of the protocol and all involved parties, to give developers a starting point to experiment and test the viability of post-quantum cryptography in current hardware devices providing FIDO2 authentication.
This project uses the None [4] Trussed framework , which is also powers None [5] Nitrokey 3 devices . Nitrokey GmbH supported this project by providing development support and tools. This achievement once again demonstrates the maturity and flexibility of the Trussed framework.
Does this mean that Nitrokey 3 will support post-quantum cryptography? No, at least not in the near future, because the FIDO specification doesn't cover post-quantum algorithms yet. Also, there are many other pieces involved, such as operating systems, web browsers, web sites, that would need to support such a future standard, or at least not conflict with it.
Read more in None [6] SandboxAQ's article .
About: SandboxAQ is an enterprise SaaS company, providing solutions at the nexus of AI and Quantum technology (AQ) to address some of the world's most challenging problems. The company's core team and inspiration formed at Alphabet Inc., emerged as an independent, growth-capital-backed company in 2022.

Does this mean that Nitrokey 3 will support post-quantum cryptography? No, at least not in the near future, because the FIDO specification doesn't cover post-quantum algorithms yet. Also, there are many other pieces involved, such as operating systems, web browsers, web sites, that would need to support such a future standard, or at least not conflict with it.
Read more in None [6] SandboxAQ's article .

The company's core team and inspiration formed at Alphabet Inc., emerged as an independent, growth-capital-backed company in 2022.

Thank you for reaching out. We'd be happy to assist you with the latency spikes you're experiencing.

To help us pinpoint the cause, could you please provide us with the following information:

* The specific API endpoint(s) affected.
* Any recent changes made to your API or infrastructure.

Once we have this information, we can investigate further and provide you with tailored recommendations.

Best regards,
The Aetheros Support Team

Gummis zur Gewichtsreduktion, die auf natürliche Weise Fett verbrennen

Was ist, wenn Sie einen langsamen Stoffwechsel haben oder Ihr ganzes Leben lang mit Gewichtszunahme zu kämpfen haben? Kein Problem.


Auch wenn Sie einen langsamen Stoffwechsel haben. Oder wenn Sie Ihr ganzes Leben lang Schwierigkeiten hatten, Gewicht zu verlieren. Oder auch, wenn Sie für eine bevorstehende Hochzeit oder ein großes Event viel Fett verlieren möchten.

Die Formel – KETO-XS – könnte dabei helfen


1 - Helfen Sie dabei, Fett als Ihre Hauptenergiequelle zu verbrennen.
2 - Steigern Sie Energie und Kraft.
3 - Erhöhen Sie die Stoffwechselrate.
4 - Um die Muskeln schneller mit Nährstoffen zu versorgen.
5 - fördert die Produktion von Proteinen in Adipozyten, die den Appetit unterdrücken. 6 - Helfen Sie dabei, Giftstoffe zu beseitigen, die sich im Laufe der Jahre angesammelt haben.

Subject: Issues with Data Analytics
Body: Facing issues with the outputs of data analytics, which are affecting the optimization of investments. The problem might be due to incompatible versions of Wolfram Mathematica and Bitbucket. So far, I have tried updating the software and verifying data integrity, but the issues still persist. I would greatly appreciate guidance on how to resolve this issue to ensure accurate data analysis. Please let us know the next steps to resolve the problem.
text:
Issues with Data Analytics

Facing issues with the outputs of data analytics, which are affecting the optimization of investments. The problem might be due to incompatible versions of Wolfram Mathematica and Bitbucket. So far, I have tried updating the software and verifying data integrity, but the issues still persist. I would greatly appreciate guidance on how to resolve this issue to ensure accurate data analysis. Please let us know the next steps to resolve the problem.

Subject: MiniNTK 2002-08-16
Body: __ __ _2002-08-16 _ _____ _ __| \/ (_)_ __ (_) \ | |_ _| |/ / o Join! mail an empty message to| |\/| | | '_ \| | \| | | | | ' / o ntknow-subscribe@lists.ntk.net| | | | | | | | | |\ | | | | . \ o Website (+ archive) lives at:|_| |_|_|_| |_|_|_| \_| |_| |_|\_\ o >> MINI NEWS << time for a snooze There comes a time in every August when news services should just shut the hell up, because the weather's too hot or too underwater, and the world grows so dull and hazy that even the overexcitable hardcore at LinuxWorld wander around like it's Undead Expo 2002, and the Weiss-baked Macintoxins can barely lift their wallets for MacOS X.2, and Sun can't be bothered to do anything but run s/RedHat/Sun Linux/g on a free distribution disk, and everyone else is on holiday, or getting ready to be on holiday, or being told that perhaps they should take a holiday RIGHT NOW, and all we can do is look at this awful, awful EUCD legislation and stare out the window until dawn. Yeah, it's our annual no news edition. We moved to a faster server. Do take the rest of the weekend off in celebration. Or, alternatively, build up a head of overexcitement for the public appearance of THOMAS DOLBY - the "Hyperactive" chart artist and Beatnik audio plug-in advocate who, even as you read this, appears to be hosting a 350 Euro-per-head seminar on COMPOSING POLYPHONIC RING TONES at Helsinki's Radisson Royal Hotel in Finland. Lucky delegates will learn about "using real-time MIDI controllers for tweaked effects", "techniques for making little speakers sound huge" and - perhaps most distressingly - "picking the right instrument and register for best audibility". The cacophonous cavalcade comes to London's Soho House on Tue and Wed next week if you'd like to check it out for yourself - or perhaps hold a street protest on behalf of music lovers worldwide. - So they're better than monophonic ringtones. What isn't? >> ANTI-NEWS << berating the obvious because there are still a few PUERILE GOOGLE MISSPELLINGS that NTK doesn't come top of the search results for - yet: , "anals of history", "blackpoo", "farternity", "proffesional" - the latter particularly in conjunction with "proofreading": ... "Subie" called "ya ,jhg hjg yjg" by unashamedly Glaswegian DAILY RECORD: ... looks like his official title now becomes "President Buh": ... frickin' MAC users buy anything: ... maybe not the best month for this branch of the W3C: ... >> EVENT QUEUE << goto's considered non-harmful Boasting technical seminars, interplay of technological ideas and, of course, beer, this year's LINUXBIERWANDERUNG (from Sat 2002-08-24, Doolin, Co. Clare, Ireland, for a "small voluntary donation" per hiker) might just be the only walking holiday you ever persuade your company to pay for. Continuing the outdoor theme, somewhat shorter notice for this Sunday's GREATER LONDON LINUX USER GROUP BLETCHLEY PARK PICNIC (around 12noon, Sun 2002-08-18, Bletchley, near Milton Keynes, UKP6 admission fee). And for anyone who can't make the whole week in Ireland, there's some consolation in the form of LONDON PERLMONGERS PRESENT: DAMIEN CONWAY AT THE CONWAY HALL (6pm, Tue 2002-08-27 and Thu 2002-08-29, Conway Hall, London WC1R, free) - hopefully just the start of a similarly-named speaker/ venue trend which will also see Larry Wall performing at the Great Wall Of China, and Eric Raymond at London's Raymond Revue Bar. - sadly, less excuse for "pingtime for Hitler" gag this year - bring your "Cryptonomicon", please don't deface his Wiki - last time: - Plug'n'Play this Sun, feat. Casiotone & Thorsten Sideboard - and Hacking @ ICA tonight, assuming you get this in time >> TRACKING << sufficiently advanced technology : the gathering Dijkstra finally gone? Great. Now we can hang out with those sloppy little unrigorous languages he never let into the house. Right on cue: the latest dev version of GNU SMALLTALK, that dirty treat-me-like-an-object ducky. Version 2.0b sprouts HTML-embedding and experimental MySQL support - implying you might actually be able to do something useful with it. Admittedly, "useful" has not been a word much used in the Smalltalk community recently. Many of the current addicts seem to live entirely in the pleasuredomes of that Land of Infinite Fun, Alan Kay's Smalltalk sequel SQUEAK, the cross-platform UI-kablooie playground which, over time, been overloaded with more ill-fitting metaphors than this sentence. But even Squeak is showing signs of returning to planet Earth. Talk of cleaning up the object tree are rife; coders are working on XP-alike skins (instead of Squeak's peculiarly homely interface right now). There's even word of a SmallTalkish scripting language in the offing. And all those new MacOS X Objective C hackers are following the trail back to the NeXT's great ancestor, giving a timely boost to the tribe. Xerox Parc *will* return! - the smaller Smalltalk - pretty colours - where they're all hiding - look! free ST books! - look! free Palm IDE! >> MEMEPOOL << ceci n'est pas une where's Wendy?: ... got a bargain from: - still, makes a change from press releases from BT OPENWORLD: ... life imitates (from last summer); in the meantime, we can look forward to: ... at last - Java PARADROID: ... so has "10,000 Statistically Grammar-Average Fake Band Names" been taken?: ... UNWITTING DOUBLE-ENTENDRE CORNER - "Genetic Distribution of the Labial Flap": vs a trained eye on "The lines, crevices, and folds of your fanny": ... >> GEEK MEDIA << get out less TV>> the objection that "Elvis - was a hero to most, but he never meant shit to me" is addressed in ENTERTAINING ELVIS (1.35am, Fri, ITV), in which "The King" offers his views on our modern-day pop acts... the horror continues in the form of CANDYMAN II (1.10am, Fri, C4) and William Peter "The Exorcist" Blatty's psycho-nonsense THE NINTH CONFIGURATION (2.45am, Fri, C4)... and Jason "Seinfeld" Alexander plays one of a bunch of gay friends - not that there's anything wrong with that - in LOVE! VALOUR! COMPASSION! (12.40am, Fri, C5)... Jarvis Cocker *is* Rolf Harris, while "Allstars" impersonate the recently deceased "Steps" in CELEBRITY STARS IN THEIR EYES (8.30pm, Sat, ITV)... Sarah Michelle Gellar and Selma "Legally Blonde" Blair put the "les" back into "Les Liaisons Dangereuses" in their noted classic CRUEL INTENTIONS (9.30pm, Sat, C4)... leading into a late- night character actor fest featuring Paul "Boogie Nights" Anderson's feature debut HARD EIGHT (12.35am, Sat, BBC2), David Mamet's Steve Martin con-game THE SPANISH PRISONER (1.20am, Sat, C4), and Oirish juvenile delinquent coming-of- ager THE BUTCHER BOY (12.55am, Sat, C5)... things get back to normal on Sunday with the incomprehensible remake of MISSION: IMPOSSIBLE (8.15pm, Sun, BBC1) - not to be confused with the simultaneously fruitless THE HUNT FOR THE ANTHRAX KILLER (9pm, Sun, BBC2)... Christina Ricci, Lisa Kudrow comedy THE OPPOSITE OF SEX (10pm, Sum, C4) has snappy script, no plot... and it's the final episode of the seemingly interminable 24 (10pm, Sun, BBC2): ... the - presumably rhetorically titled - DID BARRY GEORGE KILL JILL DANDO? (9pm, Mon, C4) and WHO KILLED SIMONE VALENTINE? (11.05pm, Mon, C4) are helpfully scheduled either either side of SIX FEET UNDER (10pm, Mon, C4)... Michael "David St Hubbins" McKean moves into management in sub-"Spinal Tap" hostage romp AIRHEADS (10.55pm, Mon, C5)... the consistently worrying TEENAGE KICKS (10pm, Tue, C4) looks at "Teenage Dwarfs" - next week: "Teenage Dirtbags"?... and inspiration BARGAIN HUNT (8pm, Thu, BBC1) arrives on prime- time - frankly we still prefer his mix of "147 Lockdown"... FILM>> if they'd used the proper mathematical sigma symbol in the title, then the sequel could have been called "The Derivative Of Distress - With Respect To Terror" for largely humourless feelgood nuclear thriller THE SUM OF ALL FEARS ( : We see [Bridget "Coyote Ugly" Moynahan] in bed, showing some cleavage; the camera briefly focuses on some cheerleaders' clothed butts during the Super Bowl)... oh and it's the full release this week for SPY KIDS 2: THE ISLAND OF LOST DREAMS ( : telepathy; pelvic thrusts by a child; drinking with multiple drugging; "Let's kick their [posteriors]" - which is not included in the list of three/four letter word vocabulary but is impudent; toilet humor - smothering in a pile of dung with fecal matter in mouth)... CONFECTIONERY THEORY>> "Are you tracking breakfast cereal?" inquired a concerned LLOYD WOOD, after recently wandering into the "kiddy end" of the cereal aisle. "They've all gone CD-ROM on the front", he observed - causing him, for a moment to think he was looking at "some really bloated computer magazines". Of course we are, Lloyd, though we're not as easily swayed by gimmicks as you are, and thus can impartially report that NESTLE's Maryland-style COOKIE CRISP are the most biscuit-like breakfast ever, easily triumphing over KELLOGG's FROSTIES CHOCOLATE (too much chocolate, not enough "frosty"), COCO POPS CRUNCHERS (clump together in this humid weather) and even the Winnie The Pooh-themed DISNEY HUNNY B's - and if you mix them all together, the milk goes so chocolatey it'll turn your Pooh brown... elsewhere in dairy, MCDONALD'S continue a disappointing run of toppings for the MCFLURRY (99p) - Cadbury's Caramel is rubbish, and Jammie Dodgers not a patch on our second-favourite McFlurry of all time, the Strawberry Crunch: . Reader ED AVIS complains that KFC don't tell you that their "M&M" AVALANCHE (also 99p) does not feature real M&Ms but "special miniaturised ones", which he describes as "frozen solid" and "might as well be small bits of gravel for all the chocolate flavour they impart". While, following last week's announcement of Ice T's Posse Pops, CRAIG LEFF thought we'd be reassured by the news that NBC are launching their own range of TV-themed Baskin-Robbins ice-creams, including a "Fear Factor" flavour designed to recreate the experience of eating "dirt and spiders": ... back with confectionery, LEON VERRALL remained unimpressed with STARBURST FRUITINESSE (from 49p, "tasted of plastic... [resembled] those 'food pills' they thought we'd all be eating by now"), DAVID BLANE grudgingly approved of the TWIX TRIPLE CHOC LIMITED EDITION ("Not bad, faintly evoking Penguins, which I never liked because of the thick chocolate"), and the whole point of that controversial "It's not for girls" campaign was revealed: to position NESTLE DOUBLE CREAM (40p) as a more female-friendly version of the YORKIE BAR. Other treats to look forward to in coming weeks: Cola-flavoured CADBURY-TREBOR REFRESHERS, chicken-wings-flavoured HULA HOOPS, bacon QUAVERS, the (presumably) non-crisp-like GOLDEN WONDER FRUIT WONDERS - or, indeed, any of this weird new American stuff: , ranging from SCORNED WOMAN CHOCOLATE JALAPENO FUDGE to SOFT AND CHEWY STINKY FEET ("Mmm, these feet are *so* stinky")... >> SMALL PRINT << Need to Know is a useful and interesting UK digest of things that happened last week or might happen next week. You can read it on Friday afternoon or print it out then take it home if you have nothing better to do. It is compiled by NTK from stuff they get sent. Registered at the Post Office as "yeah, thanks Edge, knew you had a worse picture somewhere" (p014, Edge, September 2002) NEED TO KNOW THEY STOLE OUR REVOLUTION. NOW WE'RE STEALING IT BACK. text:
MiniNTK 2002-08-16

__ __ _2002-08-16 _ _____ _ __| \/ (_)_ __ (_) \ | |_ _| |/ / o Join! mail an empty message to| |\/| | | '_ \| | \| | | | | ' / o ntknow-subscribe@lists.ntk.net| | | | | | | | | |\ | | | | . \ o Website (+ archive) lives at:|_| |_|_|_| |_|_|_| \_| |_| |_|\_\ o >> MINI NEWS << time for a snooze There comes a time in every August when news services should just shut the hell up, because the weather's too hot or too underwater, and the world grows so dull and hazy that even the overexcitable hardcore at LinuxWorld wander around like it's Undead Expo 2002, and the Weiss-baked Macintoxins can barely lift their wallets for MacOS X.2, and Sun can't be bothered to do anything but run s/RedHat/Sun Linux/g on a free distribution disk, and everyone else is on holiday, or getting ready to be on holiday, or being told that perhaps they should take a holiday RIGHT NOW, and all we can do is look at this awful, awful EUCD legislation and stare out the window until dawn. Yeah, it's our annual no news edition. We moved to a faster server. Do take the rest of the weekend off in celebration. Or, alternatively, build up a head of overexcitement for the public appearance of THOMAS DOLBY - the "Hyperactive" chart artist and Beatnik audio plug-in advocate who, even as you read this, appears to be hosting a 350 Euro-per-head seminar on COMPOSING POLYPHONIC RING TONES at Helsinki's Radisson Royal Hotel in Finland. Lucky delegates will learn about "using real-time MIDI controllers for tweaked effects", "techniques for making little speakers sound huge" and - perhaps most distressingly - "picking the right instrument and register for best audibility". The cacophonous cavalcade comes to London's Soho House on Tue and Wed next week if you'd like to check it out for yourself - or perhaps hold a street protest on behalf of music lovers worldwide. - So they're better than monophonic ringtones. What isn't? >> ANTI-NEWS << berating the obvious because there are still a few PUERILE GOOGLE MISSPELLINGS that NTK doesn't come top of the search results for - yet: , "anals of history", "blackpoo", "farternity", "proffesional" - the latter particularly in conjunction with "proofreading": ... "Subie" called "ya ,jhg hjg yjg" by unashamedly Glaswegian DAILY RECORD: ... looks like his official title now becomes "President Buh": ... frickin' MAC users buy anything: ... maybe not the best month for this branch of the W3C: ... >> EVENT QUEUE << goto's considered non-harmful Boasting technical seminars, interplay of technological ideas and, of course, beer, this year's LINUXBIERWANDERUNG (from Sat 2002-08-24, Doolin, Co. Clare, Ireland, for a "small voluntary donation" per hiker) might just be the only walking holiday you ever persuade your company to pay for. Continuing the outdoor theme, somewhat shorter notice for this Sunday's GREATER LONDON LINUX USER GROUP BLETCHLEY PARK PICNIC (around 12noon, Sun 2002-08-18, Bletchley, near Milton Keynes, UKP6 admission fee). And for anyone who can't make the whole week in Ireland, there's some consolation in the form of LONDON PERLMONGERS PRESENT: DAMIEN CONWAY AT THE CONWAY HALL (6pm, Tue 2002-08-27 and Thu 2002-08-29, Conway Hall, London WC1R, free) - hopefully just the start of a similarly-named speaker/ venue trend which will also see Larry Wall performing at the Great Wall Of China, and Eric Raymond at London's Raymond Revue Bar. - sadly, less excuse for "pingtime for Hitler" gag this year - bring your "Cryptonomicon", please don't deface his Wiki - last time: - Plug'n'Play this Sun, feat. Casiotone & Thorsten Sideboard - and Hacking @ ICA tonight, assuming you get this in time >> TRACKING << sufficiently advanced technology : the gathering Dijkstra finally gone? Great. Now we can hang out with those sloppy little unrigorous languages he never let into the house. Right on cue: the latest dev version of GNU SMALLTALK, that dirty treat-me-like-an-object ducky. Version 2.0b sprouts HTML-embedding and experimental MySQL support - implying you might actually be able to do something useful with it. Admittedly, "useful" has not been a word much used in the Smalltalk community recently. Many of the current addicts seem to live entirely in the pleasuredomes of that Land of Infinite Fun, Alan Kay's Smalltalk sequel SQUEAK, the cross-platform UI-kablooie playground which, over time, been overloaded with more ill-fitting metaphors than this sentence. But even Squeak is showing signs of returning to planet Earth. Talk of cleaning up the object tree are rife; coders are working on XP-alike skins (instead of Squeak's peculiarly homely interface right now). There's even word of a SmallTalkish scripting language in the offing. And all those new MacOS X Objective C hackers are following the trail back to the NeXT's great ancestor, giving a timely boost to the tribe. Xerox Parc *will* return! - the smaller Smalltalk - pretty colours - where they're all hiding - look! free ST books! - look! free Palm IDE! >> MEMEPOOL << ceci n'est pas une where's Wendy?: ... got a bargain from: - still, makes a change from press releases from BT OPENWORLD: ... life imitates (from last summer); in the meantime, we can look forward to: ... at last - Java PARADROID: ... so has "10,000 Statistically Grammar-Average Fake Band Names" been taken?: ... UNWITTING DOUBLE-ENTENDRE CORNER - "Genetic Distribution of the Labial Flap": vs a trained eye on "The lines, crevices, and folds of your fanny": ... >> GEEK MEDIA << get out less TV>> the objection that "Elvis - was a hero to most, but he never meant shit to me" is addressed in ENTERTAINING ELVIS (1.35am, Fri, ITV), in which "The King" offers his views on our modern-day pop acts... the horror continues in the form of CANDYMAN II (1.10am, Fri, C4) and William Peter "The Exorcist" Blatty's psycho-nonsense THE NINTH CONFIGURATION (2.45am, Fri, C4)... and Jason "Seinfeld" Alexander plays one of a bunch of gay friends - not that there's anything wrong with that - in LOVE! VALOUR! COMPASSION! (12.40am, Fri, C5)... Jarvis Cocker *is* Rolf Harris, while "Allstars" impersonate the recently deceased "Steps" in CELEBRITY STARS IN THEIR EYES (8.30pm, Sat, ITV)... Sarah Michelle Gellar and Selma "Legally Blonde" Blair put the "les" back into "Les Liaisons Dangereuses" in their noted classic CRUEL INTENTIONS (9.30pm, Sat, C4)... leading into a late- night character actor fest featuring Paul "Boogie Nights" Anderson's feature debut HARD EIGHT (12.35am, Sat, BBC2), David Mamet's Steve Martin con-game THE SPANISH PRISONER (1.20am, Sat, C4), and Oirish juvenile delinquent coming-of- ager THE BUTCHER BOY (12.55am, Sat, C5)... things get back to normal on Sunday with the incomprehensible remake of MISSION: IMPOSSIBLE (8.15pm, Sun, BBC1) - not to be confused with the simultaneously fruitless THE HUNT FOR THE ANTHRAX KILLER (9pm, Sun, BBC2)... Christina Ricci, Lisa Kudrow comedy THE OPPOSITE OF SEX (10pm, Sum, C4) has snappy script, no plot... and it's the final episode of the seemingly interminable 24 (10pm, Sun, BBC2): ... the - presumably rhetorically titled - DID BARRY GEORGE KILL JILL DANDO? (9pm, Mon, C4) and WHO KILLED SIMONE VALENTINE? (11.05pm, Mon, C4) are helpfully scheduled either either side of SIX FEET UNDER (10pm, Mon, C4)... Michael "David St Hubbins" McKean moves into management in sub-"Spinal Tap" hostage romp AIRHEADS (10.55pm, Mon, C5)... the consistently worrying TEENAGE KICKS (10pm, Tue, C4) looks at "Teenage Dwarfs" - next week: "Teenage Dirtbags"?... and inspiration BARGAIN HUNT (8pm, Thu, BBC1) arrives on prime- time - frankly we still prefer his mix of "147 Lockdown"... FILM>> if they'd used the proper mathematical sigma symbol in the title, then the sequel could have been called "The Derivative Of Distress - With Respect To Terror" for largely humourless feelgood nuclear thriller THE SUM OF ALL FEARS ( : We see [Bridget "Coyote Ugly" Moynahan] in bed, showing some cleavage; the camera briefly focuses on some cheerleaders' clothed butts during the Super Bowl)... oh and it's the full release this week for SPY KIDS 2: THE ISLAND OF LOST DREAMS ( : telepathy; pelvic thrusts by a child; drinking with multiple drugging; "Let's kick their [posteriors]" - which is not included in the list of three/four letter word vocabulary but is impudent; toilet humor - smothering in a pile of dung with fecal matter in mouth)... C18 Dentro do princípio da continuidade da classifi cação, a revisão da CNAE introduziu alterações apenas onde eram relevantes ou para manter a compara-orreiMarina seguiu como um autômato ao William Duncan. Foi a última em sair do despacho,os

Olá,

Identificamos que ao Jax de seu corpo. Começaram um baile rítmico, Jax metendo-se em sua vagina enquanto Willseu Olhem isto! Olhem o que encontrei no bosque! Podia ler a satisfação nopedido está retiQuadro 4 - variáveis dependentes do estudo: valor da empresa e desempenhodo na a- Devo voltar lhe disse quando ela fechou sua mão em torno de seulnacional dotado de claras prerrogativas soberanas sobre um territóriofRen podia ser salvo, mas havia visto como os Protetores podiam dividir os Guardiões.ânão será desagradável. Posso assegurar que não desejará pensar quenO Sr. Barlow veio até nós, os braços cruzados em fronte do seu peito de barril. Eudespalhar tudinho! Vi tudo, assisti tudo e não estou pronta para sofrer essas humilhações,ega devido Rebecca, disse uma voz masculina. Eu me afastei do inarticulado sorriso afetado que sea taxas pendentes de pagamento.

Para antes que minha avó sinta falta, supõe-se que deveria estar na casa bordando o enxoval que develiberar seu Podem se manifestar tanto no corpo físico como permanecer no terreno dopedido, por dadeira, que é encarada como uma pessoa diferente. Assim, o contofavor, morno em sua garganta. Resulta muito duro sentir-se apunhalado pelo ciúmes. É umarealize o pquentes caíram umedecendo meu rosto. "Se eu perder você, eu perco tudo", ele murmurou.aMelissa olhou para sua amiga. Você não vai saber quem ele é, o que isso importa?grelógio de seu pai, rasgada pela aflição e a confusão. Era a mesmaadecidiu se casar e ter filhos. Brogan, seu irmão mais velho, sugeriu Brigdhe. Era bonita, pertencia a umamenA compreensão golpeou a Aurora com a força de uma bofetada. Esses homenstJustin não soube avaliar se o tom de voz do Brandon era de admiração ou decepção para seumãos nos bolsos, senti algo roçar contra os meus dedos. Retirei um pequeno pedaço de papel-o o mais rápido possível.

decidi que prefiro voltar por terra. Cliff pode zarpar com você aClique no botão abaixo para efetuar o pagamento:

leu a notícia toda e ficou perturbado. Se é que interpretei bemPagaEla não gostou de sentir como se tivesse acabado de achar uma peça do quebra-cabeças,r Agora

Corcachorrinho iludido à espera de um pedaço. Esse pedaço tem que dar a seuPelos deuses, moça disse com voz rouca. Não se aproxime de mim dessardescrever o fenômeno religioso e seus diversos elementos componentes. Oeisair do pergaminho para apossar-se de seu coração. Body: eBay sent this message to your registered email address. Your registered name is included to show this message originated from eBay. Learn more. FPA NOTICE: eBay Registration Suspension - User Agreement - Abusing eBay Dear eBay Community Member, We regret to inform you that your eBay account has been suspended due to concerns we have for the safety and integrity of the eBay community. "Abusing eBay" of the eBay User Agreement states, in part: "...we may limit, suspend, or terminate our service and user accounts, prohibit access to our website, remove hosted content, and take technical and legal steps to keep users off the Site if we think that they are creating problems, possible legal liabilities, or acting inconsistently with the letter or spirit of our policies." Due to the suspension of this account, please be advised you are prohibited from using eBay in any way. This includes the registering of a new account. To confirm that you are the righfull owner of the account please confirm your identity by signing in and resolving this dispute at: Please note that any seller fees due to eBay will immediately become due and payable. eBay will charge any amounts you have not previously disputed to the billing method currently on file. Confirm your identity at the following link we provided signing in and resolving this dispute: Regards, Safeharbor Department eBay, Inc. Learn how you can protect yourself from spoof (fake) emails at: administrative email was sent to you from eBay. Your account is registered on www.ebay.com. As outlined in our User Agreement, eBay will periodically send you information about site changes and enhancements. If you would like to receive this email in text format, change your notification preferences. See our Privacy Policy and User Agreement if you have questions about eBay's communication policies. Privacy Policy: Agreement:

Subject: Concern About Project Schedule
Body: Greetings Customer Support, I am contacting you to address a serious problem with our project schedule. Lately, our project schedule has become dysfunctional, causing the loss of all team tasks. We suspect that this may be related to an incompatibility with Adobe Premiere Pro 2021. Despite restarting PostgreSQL and troubleshooting the Joystick settings, our efforts have not succeeded in fixing the issue. We would be extremely grateful if you could provide us with your assistance to resolve this as quickly as possible. Kindly inform us if there is any extra information you need from us to help with the solution. Thank you for your understanding and cooperation. Sincerely, [Your Name]
text:
Concern About Project Schedule

Greetings Customer Support, I am contacting you to address a serious problem with our project schedule. Lately, our project schedule has become dysfunctional, causing the loss of all team tasks. We suspect that this may be related to an incompatibility with Adobe Premiere Pro 2021. Despite restarting PostgreSQL and troubleshooting the Joystick settings, our efforts have not succeeded in fixing the issue. We would be extremely grateful if you could provide us with your assistance to resolve this as quickly as possible. Kindly inform us if there is any extra information you need from us to help with the solution. Thank you for your understanding and cooperation. Sincerely, [Your Name]

Subject: Morning Distribution for Wednesday, May 17, 2023
Body: A email Morning Distribution Wednesday, May 17, 2023 Your daily briefing from FiveThirtyEight The Morning Story In Red States, ‘Gun Reform’ Means Making It Easier To Buy And Carry Guns By Monica Potts So far this year, mass shootings are on the rise. So are laws expanding gun rights. This year, President Biden has worked to expand background checks and asked Congress to ban assault weapons and high-capacity magazines. But at the state level, many legislatures are moving in the opposite direction. At least 17 states, most of them led by Republicans, introduced bills this year trying to make it easier to buy, own and carry weapons, providing guns to teachers and declaring themselves Second Amendment sanctuaries, according to a FiveThirtyEight analysis. This continues a trend of Republican legislatures and governors increasing access to guns. “Many of these people … actually do believe that more guns [mean] people are safer,” said Sean Holihan, the state legislative director for Giffords, the nonprofit founded by former Arizona Rep. Gabby Giffords that aims to stop gun violence. “The data shows us over and over again that that’s simply not the case.”Read more Weekly ListenPolitics Podcast: Why So Many Americans Trust The Weather Channel View in browserABC News Unsubscribe Our mailing address: FiveThirtyEight,47 West 66th Street, New York, NY 10023.

Subject: Good news and rewards for the XRP Community
Body:
XRP Token Allocation and Onboarding Program starting Today

Ripple's Landmark Victory in the SEC Lawsuit has enabled the Foundation to share out its reserve XRP tokens to individual investors, pre-mine buyers, holders and even those just starting and funding new accounts.

 

Head on and read all the required info and program details on our latest blog post.
 

15 to 30% more XRP instantly added to your account balance
 

With a guaranteed boost of 15% additional tokens on top of the funding balance, increasing and topping up at 30% for the most experienced XRPL users, this 30-day limited offering will definitely bring more growth to our ecosystem while also boosting usage.
 

Allocations are based on every account recorded details, such as age, transactional activity, trading, trust line interaction, NFT involvement (minting, storing, or trading) and overall network participation.
 

 

Register and submit your claim with the Token Allocation Tool

 

Access the Allocation Tool, connect to RippleNet, verify your account address, estimate your reward, submit the token claim and receive your share instantly.

 

Register

Subject: Monday Motivation
Body:
You Are Not a Diagnosis: Why Menopause Doesn't Define What Your Body Can Do

Welcome to the positive corner of the internet. Today’s Health Upgrade includes:

- Arnold’s Corner: Monday Motivation
- Does lifting weights help you sleep?
- A natural way to lower blood pressure
- Workout of the week

Arnold’s Corner discusses how menopause should not define a woman's limits in fitness and strength training. The message emphasizes identity beyond diagnosis, consistency in training, and long-term strength development. Several personal stories highlight women in their 50s, 60s, 70s and beyond who improved strength, body composition, and overall confidence through resistance training.

The newsletter also reviews research showing resistance training improves sleep quality, with one study indicating participants slept approximately 40 minutes longer per night after 12 months of weight training.

Another section discusses observational research linking sun exposure to lower odds of hypertension, potentially due to nitric oxide release and vitamin D production.

The email concludes with a structured workout plan and editorial transparency regarding research standards and partnerships.

Update your email preferences or unsubscribe.

Seattle, WA 98104-2205, United States

Subject: Improve IFTTT Integration for Project Management SaaS
Body: Requesting an enhancement to the integration capabilities between IFTTT and our existing project management SaaS platform. This would significantly boost workflow automation and productivity. Currently, our team heavily relies on manual workarounds to connect the IFTTT project management tool, which is time-consuming and prone to errors. Enhanced integration would enable us to streamline tasks, reduce manual labor, and focus on higher-value activities. We would greatly appreciate it if you could consider our request and provide an update on the potential timeline for implementation. Please provide more details on the current workarounds and specific features you would like to see enhanced during our discussion.
text:
Improve IFTTT Integration for Project Management SaaS

Requesting an enhancement to the integration capabilities between IFTTT and our existing project management SaaS platform. This would significantly boost workflow automation and productivity. Currently, our team heavily relies on manual workarounds to connect the IFTTT project management tool, which is time-consuming and prone to errors. Enhanced integration would enable us to streamline tasks, reduce manual labor, and focus on higher-value activities. We would greatly appreciate it if you could consider our request and provide an update on the potential timeline for implementation. Please provide more details on the current workarounds and specific features you would like to see enhanced during our discussion.

Subject: Re: server is rejecting mail from single domain
Body: Jonathan Dill wrote: > James D. Parra wrote: >> I can suggest that to him, but this is a client so I can only ask. >> From the >> client's vantage point, our server is the only one rejecting his >> mail, so I >> need to tread politely. >> >> Concerning removing reject_non_fqdn_hostname (or >> reject_non_fqdn_helo_hostname) is not something I want to do. If this >> weren't a client of ours I wouldn't worry too much about it, but it >> is and I >> must. Is there a way could add an exception to the main.cf for this >> domain >> or IP address? >> > > If he sent e-mail to me, my server would also reject it, then you > would not be alone. But seriously, I wonder if setting up > /etc/nsswitch.conf to check "files" first and adding an entry to > /etc/hosts would fix it e.g. > > 64.22.240.195 ns.ky3.com No. don't break your system to try fixing that of someone else. Instead: - use the opportunity to put "pressure" - whitelist his IP > > I am not sure how postfix verifies fqdn, if it uses the OS resolver > stack or some other method. > > Jonathan
text:
Re: server is rejecting mail from single domain

Jonathan Dill wrote: > James D. Parra wrote: >> I can suggest that to him, but this is a client so I can only ask. >> From the >> client's vantage point, our server is the only one rejecting his >> mail, so I >> need to tread politely. >> >> Concerning removing reject_non_fqdn_hostname (or >> reject_non_fqdn_helo_hostname) is not something I want to do. If this >> weren't a client of ours I wouldn't worry too much about it, but it >> is and I >> must. Is there a way could add an exception to the main.cf for this >> domain >> or IP address? >> > > If he sent e-mail to me, my server would also reject it, then you > would not be alone. But seriously, I wonder if setting up > /etc/nsswitch.conf to check "files" first and adding an entry to > /etc/hosts would fix it e.g. > > 64.22.240.195 ns.ky3.com No. don't break your system to try fixing that of someone else. Instead: - use the opportunity to put "pressure" - whitelist his IP > > I am not sure how postfix verifies fqdn, if it uses the OS resolver > stack or some other method. > > Jonathan
